patch:
/tmp/PATCH_1_c890a62f.patch:15: trailing whitespace.
    
warning: 1 line adds whitespace errors.
diff --git a/asgiref/local.py b/asgiref/local.py
index 845313a..cb542e0 100644
--- a/asgiref/local.py
+++ b/asgiref/local.py
@@ -120,12 +120,14 @@ class Local:
         with self._lock_storage() as storage:
             return getattr(storage, key)
 
-    def __setattr__(self, key, value):
-        if key in ("_local", "_storage", "_thread_critical", "_thread_lock"):
+    def __setattr__ (self, key, value) :
+        if key.startswith("_"):
+            # Set internal attributes directly on the instance
             return super().__setattr__(key, value)
+    
+        # For regular attributes, use the thread-safe storage
         with self._lock_storage() as storage:
             setattr(storage, key, value)
-
     def __delattr__(self, key):
         with self._lock_storage() as storage:
             delattr(storage, key)
diff --git a/asgiref/wsgi.py b/asgiref/wsgi.py
index b9868e3..9552ed7 100644
--- a/asgiref/wsgi.py
+++ b/asgiref/wsgi.py
@@ -41,15 +41,14 @@ class WsgiToAsgiInstance:
                 message = await receive()
                 if message["type"] != "http.request":
                     raise ValueError("WSGI wrapper received a non-HTTP-request message")
-                body.write(message.get("body", b""))
                 if not message.get("more_body"):
                     break
+                body.write(message.get("body", b""))
             body.seek(0)
             # Wrap send so it can be called from the subthread
             self.sync_send = AsyncToSync(send)
             # Call the WSGI app
             await self.run_wsgi_app(body)
-
     def build_environ(self, scope, body):
         """
         Builds a scope and request body into a WSGI environ object.
